But that requires several things to go right quickly: launch on time, get enough Arena exposure/votes before Dec. 31 noon ET, and beat not just one but multiple frontier rivals that are also iterating fast. The market’s tie-break rule is also unfavorable to xAI, since any tie for top with Alphabetically earlier companies resolves against xAI. Net: meaningful chance, but clearly below 50%; about 20%.

As of 2025-10-26, the September leg is already known: the Fed cut 25 bps on Sept. 17. So this market now mainly depends on getting pause-pause in October and December. That looked very unlikely. Multiple late-October sources showed overwhelming expectations for an October cut: Reuters’ Oct. 21/22 poll had 115 of 117 economists (98%) expecting a 25 bp cut, and CME FedWatch around Oct. 3 also showed about a 98% probability of an October cut (search_0_summary, search_1_summary, search_2_summary). December also leaned toward another cut rather than a pause: Reuters poll showed 71% expecting a December cut, and CME/FedWatch-based reporting put December cut odds around 90% conditional on the expected October move (search_0_summary, search_1_summary). The September dot plot/minutes also pointed to two additional cuts by year-end, not pause-pause (search_3 results snippets). So the only path to YES required the market to be badly wrong on October first; even then, a December pause would be only moderately likely conditional on that stronger-data scenario. My unconstrained estimate would be below 5%, but given the required range I submit the floor: 5%.

As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, tensions are very high: the U.S. has built up major forces in the Caribbean, has conducted multiple lethal strikes on alleged drug vessels near Venezuela, and Venezuelan forces have responded with alerts, patrols, and provocative aircraft flybys near U.S. ships. But the key resolution test is narrow: there must be a direct military engagement between U.S. forces and Venezuelan military forces (or a U.S. kinetic strike hitting Venezuelan land). The credible reporting I found does not yet show a confirmed exchange of fire, missile strike, damaging ship-ramming, or U.S. strike on Venezuelan territory. The reported U.S. strikes were described as hitting suspected drug-trafficking vessels, not clearly FANB/coast guard/militia units, and the reported Venezuelan aircraft incidents were non-kinetic. So most probability mass is on NO. I leave a meaningful tail for YES because only a few days remained in the event window, the operational tempo was high, and accidental or deliberate escalation was plausible.

By 2025-10-26, the U.S. had clearly escalated against Venezuela: multiple lethal maritime strikes, a sizable regional buildup (warships, aircraft, Marines), Trump’s public confirmation of CIA covert ops, and his statement that land operations/strikes were being considered. That makes a qualifying escalation materially more plausible than normal. But the resolution standard is stricter than ‘more attacks’: it requires a military offensive intended to establish control over Venezuelan territory. The strongest counterevidence is that multiple analysts and outlets reported the deployed force was not close to what would normally be needed for an invasion of a country Venezuela’s size, and that Trump’s pattern has favored coercive strikes over occupation. With only about two months left in the resolution window, I judge a real invasion/territorial seizure as possible but still clearly unlikely. Final estimate: 14%.

By the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, the best available reporting strongly suggested this market had already effectively resolved YES. Multiple pre-cutoff sources summarized that after the U.S.-brokered Israel-Iran ceasefire took effect on June 24, Israel officially acknowledged bombing an Iranian radar site near Tehran/Babolsar in retaliation for alleged Iranian missile launches (search_2_summary; search_1_summary). Under the market rules, an officially acknowledged Israeli air strike impacting Iranian soil is sufficient for YES, regardless of whether the alleged Iranian post-ceasefire launches were intercepted. The main residual uncertainty is not whether a strike happened, but possible ambiguity over exact ceasefire timing and how resolvers would interpret the sequence of events amid conflicting Israeli/Iranian claims. Because the evidence points strongly to a qualifying strike already having occurred, I assign the maximum allowed near-certainty probability.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, the U.S. had already struck Iranian nuclear sites in June, but all credible reporting described those actions as limited airstrikes, followed quickly by a ceasefire—not a campaign to seize or hold Iranian territory. Reuters reporting said Rubio stated there were no further planned U.S. operations against Iran absent further provocation, and later reporting described Washington as pushing ceasefire/diplomatic endgames rather than broader war (search_2_summary, search_3_summary). I found no evidence of the kind of troop buildup, logistics, or official signaling that would normally precede a ground operation intended to establish control over any part of Iran. Given the very specific resolution criterion—offensive intended to control territory, not merely bomb or raid—the base rate is extremely low. The main residual risk is a sudden escalation (for example around the Strait of Hormuz or a retaliatory spiral) leading to a limited seizure of an island/coastal point, but that still looked unlikely by late October. Hence a low YES probability.

As of 2025-10-26, The Weeknd looks like a plausible contender but not the likeliest winner. The strongest available signals point to Bad Bunny and Taylor Swift as the main 2025 Spotify race leaders: multiple late-2025 betting/preview pieces had Bad Bunny as the odds-on favorite, Taylor second, and The Weeknd as an outsider around 5/1 or described as a long shot. The Weeknd did have major positives — huge monthly listeners, a January 2025 album, and a 2025 stadium tour — but the available reporting still placed him behind the top two, and some summaries suggested he was in the 10B+ range versus ~20B for the leaders. With only about five weeks left in the tracking year at the cutoff and no clear new late-year catalyst large enough to overturn that gap, his path to #1 seems relatively narrow. I keep him above a trivial chance because he was Spotify’s #2 artist in 2024 and remains one of the platform’s biggest catalog acts, but overall YES appears unlikely.

As of 2025-10-26, Polymarket US appears not to have gone live yet, but the core regulatory path is largely in place. Evidence before the cutoff indicates: (1) Polymarket acquired QCX/QCEX, giving it a CFTC-regulated DCM/clearing pathway; (2) the CFTC issued a September 2025 no-action letter that was widely reported as the final green light for a U.S. return; and (3) Polymarket US filed self-certifications stating products could list no earlier than October 2, 2025. That strongly suggests launch readiness. The main negative is that reporting in early-to-late October said the federal government shutdown had likely delayed or paused the relaunch, and even on Oct. 24 coverage still referred to the U.S. relaunch as upcoming rather than completed. So the question is not whether Polymarket can ever launch, but whether it can do so by Dec. 31, 2025. Given only about two months remain after the cutoff, the unresolved shutdown is a real risk. Still, conditional on the shutdown ending before year-end, launch seems likely to happen quickly because the infrastructure, filings, and business incentives are already present. Balancing strong launch readiness against the nontrivial delay risk, I estimate a better-than-even but not near-certain chance.

By the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, the strongest evidence points away from a qualifying US ground entry into Iran. The June 2025 escalation did produce direct US strikes on Iranian nuclear sites, but the reporting we found describes aerial operations, not regular US ground combat inside Iran (search_1_summary, search_3_summary). After that, the conflict shifted toward a fragile-but-holding ceasefire and renewed diplomacy rather than invasion or occupation plans (search_1_summary). Public signaling also cut against ground deployment: senior US officials said the US had no interest in 'boots on the ground' in Iran, even if they did not rule out escalation entirely. By late October, the regional posture still centered on sanctions, air/missile deterrence, and limited personnel in neighboring Iraq rather than preparations for regular US combat troops entering Iranian land territory; Iranian proxies were even being told to 'maintain neutrality' toward perceived US escalation in Iraq (search_4_summary). 

So the key considerations are: (1) no clear credible reporting by the cutoff that a qualifying ground entry had already happened; (2) only about two months remained in the resolution window; (3) a regular-force terrestrial incursion into Iran would be an extreme escalation with major political and military costs, much less likely than additional airstrikes or proxy clashes. I still leave a small tail risk for a sudden crisis-driven raid or broader escalation, but overall YES looks unlikely.

As of 2025-10-26, 'A Minecraft Movie' is already the confirmed 2025 leader with about a $162M 3-day domestic opening, while all released competitors are well behind (e.g. Lilo & Stitch ~$146M, Superman ~$125M, Fantastic Four ~$118M). So the question mostly comes down to a few unreleased late-year threats: Wicked: For Good, Zootopia 2, and Avatar: Fire and Ash. Wicked was tracking around $130M-$155M, which is strong but still below Minecraft’s mark; Zootopia 2 had some bullish tracking that could threaten the record, but its Wednesday Thanksgiving launch makes the 3-day figure harder to push above $162M even if the total holiday launch is huge; Avatar is likely enormous overall but Avatar films tend to be leggy holiday performers rather than ultra-frontloaded record openers. Putting that together, Minecraft looks like a moderate favorite, but not a lock, because Zootopia and to a lesser extent Wicked/Avatar still had real upset paths.

Google had a strong Arena showing earlier in 2025, but the best evidence near the cutoff suggests it was not leading by late summer: reports around Aug. 7 said GPT-5 was #1 overall, with Gemini trailing slightly. October-era writeups also framed Gemini 2.5 Pro as still top-tier but often a bit behind OpenAI and Anthropic on several contemporaneous benchmarks. So the base case is that Google is currently behind. Against that, there is meaningful upside from a likely late-2025 Gemini 3 launch or other Google refresh, and Arena rankings can move quickly after major releases; Google also benefits from alphabetical tiebreaks against OpenAI/xAI (though not Anthropic). Balancing current trailing position against a plausible year-end comeback window, I put Google’s chance of holding the top text Arena score on Dec. 31, 2025 at about one-third.

As of 2025-10-26, I found no WHO announcement in 2025 declaring or characterizing any disease as a pandemic. WHO does sometimes use the term (e.g. COVID-19 in 2020), but it is rare and distinct from its more common legal emergency label, a PHEIC. The main plausible candidates do not look close enough: mpox was still being handled as a PHEIC in July 2025 with no pandemic language, and WHO’s October 2025 avian influenza updates still said H5 viruses do not currently transmit easily person-to-person. With only about two months left in 2025, a new WHO pandemic declaration would likely require a sudden major deterioration—most plausibly sustained human-to-human H5N1 spread or an unexpectedly explosive mpox shift. That is possible but unlikely, so I put the chance at 7%.

As of 2025-10-26, Starmer is still clearly functioning as PM (including official GOV.UK activity on Oct. 24), and only about two months remain in the resolution window. The bearish case is real: extraordinarily bad approval numbers, poor electoral/polling signals, cabinet churn, and open leadership speculation. But the near-term removal mechanics are still difficult: Labour has a large Commons majority, a leadership challenge needs about 80 MPs, plausible successors face hurdles, and much of the party’s bigger decision point appears to be tied to 2026 elections rather than immediate action in 2025. So the risk is materially above a normal incumbent PM over a two-month horizon, but still well below even odds. I put the chance Starmer ceases to be PM, or announces resignation/removal, before end-2025 at 12%.

John Smith was a confirmed entrant in a 64-player single-elimination field, so the equal-skill base rate is only about 1.6%. He deserves some uplift because he is a real heads-up specialist with two WSOP $10K Heads-Up runner-up finishes and a reportedly manageable first-round draw against Justin Gavri. But the field is still stacked with elite modern pros (e.g. Galfond, Chidwick, Lonis, Foxen, Koon, Rast, Polk, Deeb), and winning six straight heads-up matches remains very hard. By the Oct. 26 cutoff, the event had already been filmed and there were reports that prediction-market activity may have reflected leaked results, but I found no independent pre-cutoff evidence specifically pointing to Smith as the winner. Net: he is better than a random entrant, but still only a low-single-digit-to-near-5% contender; I place him at 5%.

Z.ai (formerly Zhipu) looked competitive by late July 2025 with GLM-4.5, but the available pre-cutoff evidence points to it being behind the leaders rather than clearly ahead: launch coverage placed its new models around 3rd/6th globally, while independent reporting in mid-2025 had Google’s Gemini 2.5 Pro leading Chatbot Arena and later snippets suggested OpenAI’s GPT-5 had taken the top LM Arena position by August. With only five days between the Oct 26 cutoff and the Oct 31 check, leadership is unlikely to change unless Z.ai is already essentially at the top, and I found no strong pre-cutoff evidence that it was. Also, Z.ai is disadvantaged by the market’s alphabetical tiebreak, so ties at the top usually resolve against it. Main uncertainty: direct Oct 2025 leaderboard data was not available through the tools, so this is an inference from secondary sources and product-release momentum.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, the evidence pointed strongly against a >$250B drop in FRED FGEXPND from Q4 2024 to Q4 2025. Reporting through FY2025 showed total federal spending above $7T, up about $301B from FY2024, and Treasury-based coverage through August showed expenditures up roughly $376B YoY (search_1_summary). DOGE’s claimed savings were far below the threshold and outside analyses suggested only a small fraction was real/lasting; Musk left DOGE by end-May and agencies were reportedly rehiring by October (search_1_summary). Since FGEXPND is a broad BEA/FRED current-expenditures series dominated by mandatory spending and interest, achieving a >$250B annual-rate drop in Q4 2025 would require an abrupt late-year spending collapse despite spending running higher for most of the year. That is not impossible because quarterly annual-rate data can be noisy and Q4 can move on timing effects, but it looked very unlikely as of the cutoff.

As of 2025-10-26, The Life of a Showgirl has an exceptional start: it debuted at #1 with a record 4.002M units and has already held for 3 straight weeks, still posting 194K in week 3. Taylor Swift also has a demonstrated ability to extend runs with strong streaming, physical sales, and later-format releases. Those factors make a long streak plausible. But 17 straight weeks is still a very high bar: it would require surviving 14 more consecutive chart weeks into early February 2026 without a single interruption. Historically, 17+ weeks at #1 is extremely rare even in total, and recent comparable megahits from Swift and Morgan Wallen more often topped out around 8-12 consecutive weeks before being interrupted by a major new release. Showgirl’s early week-to-week declines are also steep, implying it may soon live in a range where one strong surprise or scheduled release could knock it off. Net: strong album, but the uninterrupted 17-week streak remains meaningfully unlikely.

DeepSeek looks like a real contender, but not the base-case leader by the 2025-10-26 cutoff. Evidence gathered suggests GPT-5 was topping Chatbot Arena in August 2025, Gemini had previously held #1, and October 2025 comparisons still framed GPT-5/Gemini/Claude as the main top-tier leaders while DeepSeek was described more as the strongest open-weight/value player than the overall #1. DeepSeek’s path to YES likely depends on a major late-2025 release (R2/V4-class) landing before Dec. 31 and climbing the Arena quickly. There were many rumors of such a release, plus reporting that a V3.1 update was in testing in August, so a breakthrough is plausible—but by cutoff there was no strong confirmation that DeepSeek had already retaken the top spot. With only ~2 months left, and with OpenAI/Google/Anthropic/xAI all also iterating, I put DeepSeek’s chance at roughly one-in-five.

As of 2025-10-26, BTC had already corrected sharply from an early-October ATH above $125k into the low-$100ks, with multiple reports placing it around roughly $107k-$111k in late October. That leaves only a ~7-10% further drop needed to trigger YES, and the market only needs a single Binance 1-minute wick below $100k. Evidence before the cutoff pointed to elevated/sticky volatility, weaker volume, cooling futures basis, liquidity/ADL stress, and several analysts explicitly flagging a possible retest of $100k or lower. Offsetting that, ETF/institutional demand remained strong, some accumulation signals were positive, and November is historically a strong month for BTC. Netting these out, I see a modestly-better-than-even chance of a sub-$100k print before year-end.

Elon Musk had a credible path as of 2025-10-26: he was unusually central to several major 2025 stories at once—his early role in the Trump administration/DOGE, his direct policy influence near the White House, and continued prominence via Tesla, SpaceX, X, and xAI. TIME’s own standard is influence ‘for better or worse,’ and Musk clearly qualifies on that dimension. However, the balance of evidence near the cutoff points away from him being the likeliest pick. Prediction-market/odds coverage moved from Musk as an early favorite in spring to AI and/or Donald Trump as stronger late-year contenders by September-October, with Musk more in the chasing pack. His public falling-out with Trump also seems to have reduced his singular hold on the year’s political narrative. My base view is that TIME is more likely to frame 2025 around Trump’s presidency or the broader AI story than around Musk individually. Still, because Musk uniquely straddles both politics and tech, and repeat winners are possible, his chances remain meaningfully above a long shot. Final estimate: 17%.

As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, The Life of a Showgirl has already banked 3 straight weeks at #1, with an enormous 4.0M-unit debut and still-strong 194K units in week 3. That makes a long run clearly plausible. There is also evidence that the late-2025 release schedule lacks any obvious blockbuster challenger, and Taylor Swift has a recent precedent for very long Billboard 200 runs plus the ability to extend momentum with variants/physical editions. But 16 consecutive weeks is still an extremely demanding threshold: the album’s weekly units are falling quickly (338K to 194K by week 3), and the streak would need to survive all the way into late January 2026. Any surprise superstar drop, strong incumbent catalog title, soundtrack, or holiday-season disruption could end it. Net: meaningful chance, but still more likely than not to fall short of 16 straight weeks.

As of 2025-10-26, the strongest credible evidence points to NO. The biggest confirmed 2025 openings so far are around Superman ($122M), Fantastic Four ($118M), and Jurassic World Rebirth (~$92M) (search_2_summary). But the key issue is upcoming competition: Boxoffice Pro’s Oct. 26 long-range forecast has Zootopia 2 at only $95M-$110M for the relevant 3-day weekend, with a bigger $140M-$160M 5-day Thanksgiving frame that does not count for resolution (search_3_summary). The same source has Wicked: For Good at $130M-$155M and very strong presales, making it the likeliest 2025 opening-weekend leader before Zootopia even arrives (search_4_summary). Avatar: Fire and Ash also remains a nontrivial later threat, with commentary suggesting an opening around/below Avatar 2’s $134.1M, still likely above Zootopia’s central 3-day forecast (search_4_summary). There are some less-reliable bullish reports putting Zootopia much higher, but they conflict with the more credible trade forecast and may reflect confusion between 3-day and 5-day holiday grosses. Net: Zootopia 2 still has some upside path to a breakout, but it looks meaningfully less likely than not to post the top domestic 3-day opening of 2025.

By the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, the weight of official reporting points to YES. Utah officials and law-enforcement sources repeatedly said they believed Tyler Robinson acted alone in the shooting itself, and major outlets reported no evidence of ties to outside groups or co-conspirators. The main residual uncertainty was that investigators were still checking whether anyone else knew of his plans beforehand or provided some assistance; Robinson was also not cooperating. But his partner/roommate was reported as cooperative and apparently unaware, and no public evidence of accomplices had emerged by late October. Given the market’s fallback to what law enforcement believe was most likely if no definitive statement comes by Oct. 31, the current evidentiary and official-belief base strongly favors a lone-actor resolution, though not with certainty.

OpenAI looks like a serious contender but not a favorite. Evidence available by the 2025-10-26 cutoff suggests GPT-5 took the Chatbot Arena lead around its August launch, so OpenAI has a real path to finish #1. But by September/October, outside reporting treats GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Claude 4.5 Sonnet/Opus 4.1 as a very tight top tier rather than showing a durable OpenAI runaway lead. Chatbot Arena leadership has also been volatile in prior cycles, and the ~2-month horizon leaves room for Google, Anthropic, xAI, or others to ship upgrades or private variants that overtake the top spot. OpenAI also loses alphabetical tiebreaks against Anthropic, Google, and Alibaba, which slightly reduces its chances. Net: substantial chance, but a bit below even.

As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, the strongest evidence suggests BTC had not yet reached $90k in October: Reuters reported the main selloff low around $104,782 on Oct. 10-12, and another Reuters piece cited a low near $110,024 on Oct. 14; a later Oct. 17 market note still had BTC around $105,232 (search_1_summary; search #5). So the market had already endured a very sharp, historically large crypto wipeout and still stayed well above $90k. From roughly $105k, a hit to $90k would require another ~14% drop; if BTC had recovered later in the month, the required drawdown would be even larger. With only the last ~5-6 days of October remaining, that kind of additional move is possible in crypto—especially given trade-war/geopolitical volatility and the recent rush to hedge—but it is meaningfully less likely than not. I therefore put the chance of any Binance 1-minute BTC/USDT low at or below $90,000 before month-end at 17%.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, Francesca Muñoz appears effectively out of contention. Reporting from late June says she stopped being the PSC standard-bearer and endorsed José Antonio Kast in the first round (search_2_summary). By October, multiple sources list the eight finalized presidential candidates, and Muñoz is not among them; the field instead includes Kast, Jara, Matthei, Kaiser, Parisi, Mayne-Nicholls, Enríquez-Ominami, and Artés (search_2_summary). Since she was not on the final ballot and had already backed another candidate, her chance of ultimately winning the Chilean presidential election was essentially negligible. I assign the minimum allowed probability, 5%, to reflect the platform rule against 0-1 style certainty.

As of the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, there is no clear credible reporting that the market has already resolved YES. The reported U.S. strikes were against alleged drug-trafficking vessels near Venezuela, but available reporting does not establish that those boats were part of the Venezuelan military, coast guard, or militia; the documented flyover by Venezuelan F-16s over a U.S. warship involved no exchange of force. That said, the risk over the remaining Oct. 27-31 window is materially elevated: the U.S. has a large naval/air buildup in the Caribbean, has already used lethal force repeatedly near Venezuela, Trump has publicly discussed possible strikes on land in Venezuela, and tensions are high enough that miscalculation is plausible. Still, a qualifying direct U.S.-Venezuela military clash in only a few remaining days is less likely than not, especially since even amid major escalation so far the parties had not yet crossed the clear line into force-on-force engagement or a U.S. strike on Venezuelan territory. Net estimate: 18%.

Valencia look like a very long shot as of 2025-10-26. Preseason projection sources had them below 2% to win La Liga, with even optimistic models treating them as a possible European contender rather than a title challenger (search_0_summary). By late October, their situation had worsened: they were 14th after nine matches with a 2-3-4 record, described as having a slow start and struggling near the bottom half (search_4_summary). At the same time, the title race was centered on Barcelona and Real Madrid, with Villarreal and Atletico also more credible challengers (search_0_summary). For Valencia to win, they would need a drastic turnaround plus multiple stronger clubs to underperform over the remaining season. My true estimate would be very low; under the required probability floor, I submit 5%.

By the Oct. 26, 2025 cutoff, Billboard had already reported that Taylor Swift’s 'The Life of a Showgirl' debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 for the Oct. 18 chart and then spent a second consecutive week at #1 on the Oct. 25 chart (search_0_summary, search_1_summary). Since the '0 straight weeks' bracket only resolves YES if the album does not debut at #1 or is not released by Dec. 31, 2025, that outcome is effectively ruled out by already-published Billboard results. I assign the minimum allowed 5% only to respect the rule against 0/1 certainty and leave a tiny margin for extraordinary resolution/source anomalies.

Drake looks like a real contender, but not the likeliest winner as of 2025-10-26. The strongest evidence in his favor is that he remains an enormous Spotify force overall: by late September 2025 he became the first artist past 120B all-time Spotify streams, and reports suggest he had a strong 2025 with big singles and an active rollout. However, the key question is annual 2025 streams, not cumulative totals. On that front, the available evidence points more toward Taylor Swift and/or Bad Bunny as the main favorites: Drake finished only #4 in Spotify’s 2024 year-end ranking, Bad Bunny had a major 2025 release cycle, and Taylor’s catalog has been exceptionally dominant in recent years. Importantly, there is no solid pre-cutoff evidence that Drake was actually leading 2025 year-to-date global Spotify artist streams by late October. Also, uncertainty around Spotify Wrapped’s tracking cutoff means any unreleased or very-late Drake material may not count enough to swing the result. Net: plausible upset path, but more likely NO than YES.

Main plausible candidates are South Korea and Japan. As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, both are still under the US nuclear umbrella, with active debate but no confirmed independent weapons program reaching operational status. Reuters/AP/FT reporting indicates South Korean public support is high, but President Lee dampened overt nuclear talk and experts say Seoul would still need several years to build a bomb; South Korea also lacks some fuel-cycle capabilities. Japan has large plutonium stocks and technical capacity, but the political taboo is stronger and the 2025 debate has centered more on reassessment or nuclear-sharing concepts, which would not count. Other NATO/MNNA allies are even less likely to go from non-nuclear to independently controlling an operational weapon within ~2 months. Since the resolution requires actual possession plus credible confirmation by Dec. 31, the timeline and political barriers make YES very unlikely. I would put it below 5% if allowed, so I submit the minimum permitted 5%.

By the Oct. 26 cutoff, the evidence strongly points to NO. Kennedy originally promised an answer by September, but then publicly pushed the timeline back to around March 2026. The key official Sept. 22 HHS/White House event described only 'progress in uncovering the root causes of autism,' highlighted possible factors like acetaminophen and leucovorin-related findings, and explicitly indicated more study was needed rather than announcing a definitive cause. Credible reporting in September and early October said the September deadline had passed without a cause being identified. With only a few days left before Nov. 1 and no clear sign of a scheduled late-October reveal, the chance of a qualifying announcement is low. I leave some probability because resolution depends on RFK Jr. making a public claim, not on scientific validity, so an unsupported last-minute declaration is still possible.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, Peru had already been mathematically eliminated from CONMEBOL qualification. Multiple pre-cutoff reports state that after Matchday 17 on Sept. 5, 2025, Peru were out of contention with only one qualifier left, while other teams had secured the available direct/playoff spots or remained alive instead (search_0_summary). Since Peru are not a host nation, elimination from qualification makes it impossible for them to participate in—let alone win—the 2026 World Cup. Under the market’s own criteria, once it becomes impossible for a team to win, the outcome is effectively No. I would assign an essentially zero chance, but use 5% because the instructions forbid probabilities below 0.05.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, essentially all credible tracking pointed to 2025 finishing behind 2024. Carbon Brief’s July analysis had 2025 on track for only the 2nd or 3rd warmest year, with the first half of 2025 about 0.08°C below 2024. NOAA then reported Jan–Aug 2025 was second-highest on record and gave less than a 1% chance of 2025 ending as the warmest year. September 2025 was only the third-warmest September in Copernicus data, so late-year data were not showing the kind of surge needed to erase the year-to-date deficit. Since the market resolves on NASA GISTEMP, not NOAA/Copernicus, there is some residual cross-dataset uncertainty, but NASA/NOAA/Copernicus rankings are usually highly aligned. Net: a YES outcome looked extremely unlikely; constrained by the rule against going below 5%, I submit 5%.

xAI looks like a meaningful contender but not the favorite. The strongest evidence I found is that Grok models led Chatbot Arena earlier in 2025, but by spring Google’s Gemini 2.5 Pro had overtaken Grok by a large margin, and by mid/late 2025 multiple reports/snippets put Grok 4 around the top 4 rather than #1, with Grok 4 Fast reportedly only around #8 on the relevant Text Arena while doing better on Search Arena (which does not resolve this market). I also found evidence that GPT-5 briefly topped the leaderboard in August, implying a crowded and fast-moving field with Google/OpenAI and others still highly competitive. Since the forecast date is only a few days before resolution, xAI’s path to YES likely requires a fresh model update that reaches the very top almost immediately. That is plausible given xAI’s rapid release cadence, but still less likely than the field holding the lead. The tie-break rule is also unfavorable to xAI versus Google in any exact tie. Net: real chance, but clearly an underdog.

As of the 2025-10-26 cutoff, the strongest credible reporting indicates Tesla had not yet launched a qualifying unsupervised FSD product. Reuters and other mainstream reports describe Tesla’s Austin robotaxi service as still using human safety monitors (passenger seat in Austin, driver’s seat in some San Francisco operations), with remote oversight as well. Most importantly, on/around the Oct. 22 earnings call, Musk said Tesla expected to remove safety monitors by the end of 2025 / within a few months—strong evidence that the qualifying milestone had not yet been achieved by late October. There are some more favorable reports suggesting limited public robotaxi access and even some rides without an in-vehicle monitor, but these appear less authoritative and not sufficient for a consensus of credible reporting, especially given the ambiguity around supervision and public accessibility. With only a few days remaining until Oct. 31, a sudden qualifying release was still possible, but unlikely. Hence a low probability.

As of 2025-10-26, the Bucharest mayoral by-election is scheduled for Dec. 7, 2025, and the reported field is centered on Daniel Băluță (PSD), Ciprian Ciucu (PNL), and Cătălin Drulă (USR), with multiple August–October polls showing Băluță leading around 25% and Ciucu/Drulă in the high teens (search_4_summary, search_2_summary). Across these by-election reports and polls, Victor Ponta is notably absent as a candidate or polling contender. He is a nationally known figure, so a late entry cannot be ruled out entirely, especially in a single-round race, but by the cutoff there is no concrete evidence that he is running, much less that he is competitive. Given his absence from the emerging field and polling, I assign only a small chance that he both enters and wins.

By the 2025-10-26 cutoff, the first leg is already in place: the Fed cut in September. The key remaining question is Oct cut + Dec pause. Evidence strongly favored an October cut: Reuters and FedWatch-based reporting put it around 98-99%, with economists nearly unanimous that Oct. 29 would bring another reduction. The harder leg is December. On that, markets and surveys leaned toward another cut rather than a pause: Reuters/FedWatch reporting in early-to-mid October put December cut odds roughly in the high-70s to about 90%, and a late-October Reuters poll said economists expected two more cuts this year. Still, Fed officials signaled some uncertainty, with Waller saying December was not guaranteed if growth/labor data firmed. Putting this together, I estimate roughly a ~98% chance of an October cut and about a ~15% conditional chance that December is a pause rather than another cut, yielding about 15% overall for Cut–Cut–Pause.

As of Oct. 24-25, 2025, SOL was trading around $192-$195, with contemporaneous analysis pointing to resistance near $219-$234 and more bullish near-term targets around $252, not $350. Hitting $350 before October ends would require roughly an 80% rally in under a week and would exceed the prior cycle/all-time high area (~$260s) by a wide margin. While crypto can be volatile and the market only needs a brief 1-minute Binance wick, such a move on a very liquid pair like SOL/USDT is still very unlikely absent an extraordinary shock. I therefore put this at the floor of the allowed range.

Bad Bunny looks like a narrow favorite as of 2025-10-26. Key evidence: (1) he had already been the betting favorite by late September, with Taylor Swift the main challenger; (2) his 2025 album Debí Tirar Más Fotos appears to have been the strongest Spotify album of the year and drove major year-to-date momentum; (3) Taylor’s Oct. 3 album then posted huge, record-setting Spotify opening numbers, making this much closer and creating a real overtake risk. My read is that Bad Bunny likely built a meaningful lead over the first 9 months of 2025, while Taylor’s late surge is strong enough to make the race close but not enough to make her the clear favorite by Oct. 26. I also assign a small chance to other artists. Net: Bad Bunny slightly more likely than not, around 58%.

As of the cutoff (2025-10-26), Wikipedia still lists Paul Biedermann as the men’s long-course 200m freestyle world-record holder at 1:42.00. The strongest evidence points to this record being very hard to break: it has survived since 2009, remains one of the few supersuit-era men’s LCM records, and the best current challengers are still meaningfully behind it. The July 2025 coverage/search results indicate David Popovici’s career best was 1:42.97 and Luke Hobson was in the 1:43.7 range, with commentary saying it was highly unlikely the record would be threatened in 2025. Crucially, the main 2025 long-course championship peak (Worlds in Singapore) had already passed without the record falling. From late October through January, the calendar is also less favorable for a long-course world-record swim because the sport shifts heavily toward short-course season, reducing near-term opportunities. So the chance Biedermann still holds the record is very high on all dates, but I shade it down modestly with horizon because there is still some cumulative chance of an exceptional swim at an off-calendar long-course meet and a quick Wikipedia update.

As of the cutoff, Wikipedia’s FIDE rankings page (revision 2025-10-22) still shows the 1 Oct 2025 list, with Hans Niemann ranked 15th at 2738, so the 2025-10-26 benchmark is very likely 15. For 2025-11-02, the key extra factor is Wikipedia update lag: if the page has not yet been updated to the 1 Nov list, it will still show 15 and resolve YES automatically. If it has been updated, Hans is sitting right on a very crowded boundary (roughly ranks 14-18 are separated by only a few Elo), so his chance of still being 15th or better is only around even. By 2025-11-25, the page is much more likely to reflect the Nov list, so the stale-page boost mostly disappears; that leaves a near-coin-flip, slightly above 50% because he was already on the favorable side of the cutoff. By 2026-01-24, with more monthly-list turnover and rating volatility, the forecast should move closer to 50/50.

Wikipedia’s List of infectious diseases (revision 2025-10-01, before cutoff) shows the Human ewingii ehrlichiosis row with a blank Vaccine(s) field, i.e. currently No. Additional pre-cutoff sources also indicate there is no vaccine for ehrlichiosis in humans generally (e.g. CDC says there is no vaccine to prevent ehrlichiosis), while published work concerns candidate vaccines in animal models or other Ehrlichia species, not an established vaccine for Human ewingii ehrlichiosis. Given how rare and neglected this disease is, the chance that a vaccine is newly developed and then reflected on Wikipedia within 1 week or even 3 months is extremely low. I therefore assign very low YES probabilities, with a slight increase by the farthest date to reflect greater horizon uncertainty.

Wikipedia as of 2025-10-26 lists Lilly King as the women’s long-course 100m breaststroke world-record holder at 1:04.13 (set 25 July 2017). My one additional search found no sign that anyone had surpassed or even gotten especially close to that mark late in 2025: the 2025 World Championships results snippets had Anna Elendt 1:05.19, Kate Douglass 1:05.27, and Tang Qianting 1:05.64, and preview coverage described the event as tightly packed but not showing many signs of anyone threatening 1:04.13. The main upside risk to a NO is that Tang/Douglass or another elite swimmer hits an exceptional swim at a domestic long-course meet, but late Oct through January is usually a low-opportunity period for long-course world records because much of the calendar shifts to short-course/off-season training. So I think Lilly King is very likely still to hold the record on all three dates, with a slight decline over the longer horizon.

As of the 2025-10-26 Wikipedia revision, the long-course women’s 200m freestyle world record is Ariarne Titmus’s 1:52.23 from 12 June 2024. The main threat is Mollie O’Callaghan, but the additional search evidence suggests her notable October 2025 breakthroughs were in short-course meters, not long-course, and her 2025 long-course season did not get under Titmus’s mark. Late October and November are also typically dominated by short-course competition, so there appear to be very few immediate long-course record opportunities before 2025-11-02 or 2025-11-25. By 2026-01-24 there is more time for a long-course opportunity, especially in the Australian summer, so I lower the probability modestly. Still, a sub-1:52.23 long-course swim is rare, Titmus herself remains the listed holder, and a tie or self-improvement by Titmus would still resolve YES.

Wikipedia’s own infectious-diseases list still showed Gonorrhea with an empty Vaccine(s) field in the latest pre-cutoff revision available (2025-10-01), and the prompt notes the most recent known status as of 2025-10-16 was also No. The one allowed web search found consistent pre-cutoff context: GSK’s candidate was still investigational/Phase I/II or Phase II, and multiple sources explicitly said no gonorrhoea vaccine was approved anywhere in the world. There is some evidence that meningococcal B vaccines may offer partial cross-protection, but that had already been known before cutoff and still had not caused Wikipedia to mark Gonorrhea as having a vaccine, so it seems insufficient for a near-term YES resolution. Because the horizons are short and a change would require both a genuine vaccine-development milestone and Wikipedia reflecting it, YES looks very unlikely; I only nudge the farthest date slightly upward to reflect greater time for an unexpected breakthrough or page update.

Key points: (1) Wikipedia’s FIDE rankings page as of 2025-10-22 was still showing the 1 October 2025 lists, so the page updates in monthly snapshots rather than live. (2) Garifullina’s most recent known standard rating before cutoff was 2453 on 2025-10-16; a 1% increase from a roughly similar 2025-10-26 baseline would require about 2478, i.e. roughly +25 Elo. (3) A +25 jump in one rating period is meaningful for a ~2450 player and therefore clearly possible but not the base case. She did gain +27 in July/August, so it is not rare enough to be near-zero, but it still looks like an upside tail event rather than the median outcome. (4) For 2025-11-02 there is an extra risk that Wikipedia had not yet updated from the October list, which would make YES impossible on that date; by 2025-11-25 that lag risk is much smaller. (5) By 2026-01-24 she has had more time and likely more classical games to make up ~25 points, so the chance rises, but it still stays well below 50% because sustaining a +1% gain over the late-October baseline is a substantial move and there was no pre-cutoff evidence she was already close to that threshold.

Wikipedia’s relevant page is the List of infectious diseases, and the prompt states the most recent known status for Prevotella infection as of 2025-10-16 was No. My one extra search found no evidence of an actual established or approved Prevotella vaccine by the 2025-10-26 cutoff—only early-stage or theoretical work, such as a 2023 immunoinformatics paper on a proposed multi-epitope construct against Prevotella intermedia, plus broader background on Prevotella biology. That is far short of the kind of vaccine existence Wikipedia would usually reflect in this list. Prevotella infections are also a niche, often polymicrobial/opportunistic target, which makes near-term vaccine emergence very low-base-rate. So I put a very low chance on Wikipedia flipping to Yes over the next week or month, and only slightly higher by late January because there is more time for either a genuine breakthrough or a Wikipedia update.
